intro: Commidendrum spurium, also known as "Milk Bush" or "Commidendrum", is a small, shrub-like succulent plant native to South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential uses in treating various skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The bioactive constituents of Commidendrum spurium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Commidendrum spurium is often prepared as a topical cream or ointment, or infused as a tea to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
